title: Pascal: _lower_pascal_method stub missing DECL_VAR self alias
description: _lower_pascal_method (class body forward declaration stub) emits DECL_VAR this but not DECL_VAR self. The defProc body emits both. If Pascal code in a stub body ever references self, it would fail. Low practical risk since stubs have empty bodies, but an asymmetry worth fixing for consistency.

title: Pascal: qualified function (not procedure) defProc not tested
description: lower_pascal_proc handles both kFunction and kProcedure via genericDot detection, but only procedure TFoo.SetName is tested. Need a test for function TFoo.GetName: string to verify return type extraction works with qualified names.

title: C#: ref to array element (ref arr[i]) not supported
description: ref int x = ref arr[i]; requires constructing Pointer with computed offset. Follow-up to red-dragon-c4v.

title: C#: ref to object field (ref obj.field) not supported
description: ref int x = ref obj.field; requires constructing Pointer with field name offset. Follow-up to red-dragon-c4v.

title: C#: ref returns (return ref arr[0]) not supported
description: Returning a reference from a method. Follow-up to red-dragon-c4v (ref locals). Requires ref local support first.

title: C#: ref reassignment (x = ref arr[1]) not supported
description: Rebinding a ref local to point at a different location. Follow-up to red-dragon-c4v (ref locals). Requires ref local support first.

title: C++: structured binding struct member decomposition via LOAD_FIELD
description: auto [x, y] = point; where point is a struct — decompose into LOAD_FIELD per public member instead of LOAD_INDEX. Requires knowing field names at compile time. Follow-up to red-dragon-cq8 which only supports positional (LOAD_INDEX) decomposition.

title: C#: ref return and ref local not supported
description: C# 7.0 ref returns (ref int Find(...) => return ref arr[i]) and ref locals (ref int slot = ref Find(...)). Requires managed pointers that survive across function returns. Much more complex than out/ref/in params. Low priority unless real-world code exercises this.

title: Introduce LOAD_INDIRECT / STORE_INDIRECT opcodes to replace LOAD_FIELD/STORE_FIELD with magic "*" field
description: ADR-099 uses LOAD_FIELD ptr "*" and STORE_FIELD ptr "*" val to dereference pointers. The "*" field name is a magic string overloading field access semantics. Introduce dedicated LOAD_INDIRECT / STORE_INDIRECT opcodes with clear intent. Migrate C and Rust frontends, then C# byref params. Blocked by: red-dragon-ia8 (C# out/ref/in must land first using the existing "*" convention).

title: VM: pass-by-reference semantics for out/ref parameters
description: C# out/ref parameters require pass-by-reference: callee assigns to the parameter and the caller sees the updated value. Currently the VM passes all arguments by value, so out params keep their default (0) in the caller scope. Needs a ref-cell or address-of mechanism in the VM to support this.

title: tree-sitter-c-sharp: v * scale parsed as declaration_expression in constructor initializer args
description: tree-sitter-c-sharp 0.23.1 (latest) parses 'this(v * scale)' inside a constructor_initializer as declaration_expression instead of binary_expression (multiplication). This is a grammar bug — constructor initializer argument lists should not permit declaration_expression. Workaround: use addition or explicit this.field assignment in the constructor body. May be worth filing upstream at the tree-sitter-c-sharp repo.

title: Scala auxiliary constructor this() chaining
description: Scala auxiliary constructors call this(args) for delegation. Currently not handled. Should follow the Java/Kotlin pattern for constructor delegation.

title: C# constructor chaining via :this() — lower as CALL_METHOD like Java
description: C# supports constructor chaining with :this(args) syntax. Currently not handled. Should follow the Java pattern: emit CALL_METHOD on this with __init__ as method name.

title: Kotlin secondary constructor delegation should inline full primary constructor body (including field initializers)
description: Currently _emit_constructor_delegation only maps delegation arguments to primary constructor params via STORE_FIELD. It doesn't replay field initializer stores (e.g. var doubled: Int = 0). This means fields initialized in the class body don't exist on the heap object when the secondary constructor body runs, breaking implicit this.field resolution for those fields. Fix: inline the full primary constructor body (param stores + field initializer stores) during delegation. xfail: test_secondary_constructor_body_reads_field in test_kotlin_frontend_execution.py
close_reason: Tasks 5-6 done: Builtins.TABLE and METHOD_TABLE now FuncName-keyed, str() removed from lookup_builtin and lookup_method_builtin

title: Replace regex-based CLASS_REF_PATTERN with structured class references
description: Class references are stringly-typed (e.g. '<class:Dog@class_Dog_0:Animal>') and parsed back with CLASS_REF_PATTERN regex. Same problem as red-dragon-fdc but for classes. Replace with structured data type. Do after red-dragon-fdc is complete.

title: Replace regex-based FUNC_REF_PATTERN with structured function references
description: Function references are currently stringly-typed (e.g. '<function:new@func_new_0>') and parsed back with FUNC_REF_PATTERN regex. This is fragile — e.g. dotted names like 'Counter.new' break \w+ matching. Replace with a structured data type (dataclass or similar) that carries function name, label, and optional class directly, eliminating regex parsing entirely.

title: Lua: colon syntax (:) with implicit self for method dispatch
description: Lua colon syntax (obj:method()) implies implicit self as first argument. Currently only dot syntax is supported (ADR-104). Colon syntax should emit CALL_METHOD with implicit self injection, while dot syntax continues to use LOAD_FIELD + CALL_UNKNOWN with explicit self.

title: Pascal: unit/module system not supported
description: Pascal unit declarations (unit MyUnit; interface ... implementation ... end.) are the module system. UNIT node not dispatched. Required for multi-file Pascal analysis.

title: Java: string templates (Java 21) not supported
description: Java 21 string templates (STR."Hello \\{name}") are a new core language feature. TEMPLATE_EXPRESSION not dispatched.

title: Ruby: string interpolation node not dispatched
description: Ruby INTERPOLATION node (the \#{expr} inside double-quoted strings) is not explicitly dispatched. May be handled via parent traversal, but worth verifying.

title: C++: coroutines (co_await/co_return/co_yield) not supported
description: C++20 coroutines are increasingly common in async C++ code. CO_AWAIT_EXPRESSION, CO_RETURN_STATEMENT, CO_YIELD_STATEMENT not dispatched.

title: Rust Result/panic\! error handling not supported
description: Rust's error handling uses Result<T, E> with ? operator and panic\!/catch_unwind for unrecoverable errors. These are ubiquitous in real-world Rust code. Our VM returns SymbolicValue for these patterns. The Rust frontend should handle Result as a type (similar to Option) and support the ? operator for early error return.

title: Add string concat builtin to VM for languages with native concat functions
description: C has strcat(), Rust has format\!(), and other languages have native string concat functions beyond the + operator. The VM should support a concat builtin that these can lower to, enabling string concatenation in languages where + doesn't naturally apply to strings. Currently C is excluded from the Rosetta string_concat test because it has no expression-level concat.

title: Pascal TCounter.Create (Delphi OOP constructor) returns symbolic
description: Pascal class constructors via TCounter.Create return SymbolicValue. The class is recognized but .Create (inherited Delphi constructor) is not wired up as a constructor call. This blocks method chaining and other OOP patterns in Pascal.

title: Go defer/recover error handling not supported
description: Go's error handling uses defer/recover for panic recovery. This is a common real-world Go pattern. Our VM doesn't handle defer statements or recover() calls. The Go frontend should lower defer as a deferred function call and recover() as a builtin.

title: Lua pcall/xpcall error handling not supported
description: Lua's error handling mechanism is pcall(fn)/xpcall(fn, handler). These are real-world Lua patterns for error recovery. Our VM returns SymbolicValue for pcall calls. The Lua frontend should handle pcall as a builtin that calls the function and returns (true, result) or (false, error).

title: Java hex float literals stored as strings instead of parsed to float
description: 0x1.0p10 should parse to 1024.0 during lowering, but it's stored as the raw string '0x1.0p10'. The Java frontend's literal handler needs to recognize hex_floating_point_literal nodes and convert them via float.fromhex() or equivalent.
close_reason: Closed

title: PHP print expression should return 1
description: PHP's print is an expression that always returns 1. Our VM treats it as a statement, so $r = print 'hi' stores None instead of 1. The PHP frontend should lower print_intrinsic to emit CONST 1 as the result register after the print side-effect.

title: Register method signatures for prelude classes (Box, Option) in TypeEnvironmentBuilder
description:
The prelude classes (Box, Option) emitted by the Rust frontend bypass the normal type inference pipeline. The TypeEnvironmentBuilder never sees their method signatures, so the executor's overload resolver always hits the 'sig/label count mismatch' warning and falls back to index 0.

This works for now because prelude methods have no overloads, but it's fragile. The fix should register method signatures for prelude classes either:
1. During prelude emission (the frontend knows the signatures)
2. During TypeEnvironmentBuilder scanning (teach it to recognize prelude_class_ labels)
3. Via a prelude-specific method signature registration in the TypeEnvironment

Related: red-dragon-62g (Box/Option implementation)

title: Rust frontend: Box/Option unwrap in recursive linked list traversal returns SymbolicValue
description: Simple struct field access works (Point { x: 42 }; p.x → 42). But recursive traversal through Option<Box<Node>> (node.next_node.as_ref().unwrap()) returns SymbolicValue. The .as_ref().unwrap() chain is not resolved by the VM. Affects Rosetta linked list test — Rust is one of 2/15 languages still symbolic.

title: Pascal frontend: array-of-records index-based traversal returns SymbolicValue
description: Simple record field access works (p.x := 42; answer := p.x → 42). But array-of-records traversal (nodes[idx].value, nodes[idx].nextIdx) returns SymbolicValue. The combination of array indexing + field access on the result is not resolved. Affects Rosetta linked list test — Pascal is one of 2/15 languages still symbolic.

title: Class/struct type metadata: ClassTypeDescriptor with TypeExpr fields
description: Add first-class type metadata for class/struct definitions. Store ClassTypeDescriptor (name, fields as dict[str, TypeExpr], parent types) on the context during lowering, replacing the current approach where struct field info exists only as IR instructions. Support multi-pass resolution for recursive types (struct Node { Node* next }). Integrate with TypeEnvironmentBuilder. This enables struct-aware initializer lists, better type inference, and moves toward JVM/CLR-style type metadata. Supersedes the IR-scan approach used for the initializer list fix.

title: Pascal frontend: record field access through pointer deref returns SymbolicValue
description: Pascal record field access via pointer dereference (node^.value) returns SymbolicValue. Direct record field access (n1.value := 3; answer := n1.value) works for simple dot notation. The pointer-based traversal pattern needed for linked lists doesn't resolve. Also: the @ (address-of) operator is unsupported (lowered as 'symbolic unsupported:kAt').
notes: Pascal record field access works for direct dot notation (answer=42). Pointer deref is separate.

title: Scala frontend: primary constructor val params not lowered to STORE_FIELD
description: Scala class with primary constructor val params (class Node(val value: Int, val nextNode: Node)) generates an empty class body — no __init__ method, no STORE_FIELD instructions. The VM creates the object but fields are never set, so LOAD_FIELD returns SymbolicValue. Same issue as Kotlin (red-dragon-6cb).

title: C frontend: struct field access through pointers returns SymbolicValue
description: C struct field access works with direct dot notation (p.x = 42; answer = p.x → 42). But accessing fields through pointer dereference (node->value, node->next_node) returns SymbolicValue. Initializer-list construction ({3, 0}) also doesn't persist field values for later LOAD_FIELD resolution. Affects linked list traversal patterns.

title: Rust frontend: struct literal field access returns SymbolicValue
description: Rust struct literals (Node { value: 3, next_node: None }) create objects via call_function, but subsequent LOAD_FIELD on the object returns SymbolicValue. The struct literal initialization doesn't store fields in a way the VM can resolve. Simple field access like n1.value after construction returns sym_X instead of the concrete value.
notes: Rust struct literals already produce concrete field values (answer=42). No fix needed.

title: C++ frontend: explicit 'this' param in __init__ breaks field storage
description: C++ constructor lowering emits 'symbolic param:this' as the first parameter of __init__, but the VM injects 'this' implicitly when calling constructors. This causes parameter binding mismatch — the constructor args get shifted, and STORE_FIELD operates on a symbolic 'this' instead of the actual object reference. Result: LOAD_FIELD on C++ objects returns SymbolicValue. Java/C#/Python/JS/etc. do NOT include 'this' as a parameter and work correctly.

title: Kotlin frontend: secondary constructor syntax not lowered
description: Kotlin secondary constructors (constructor(v: Int) { this.value = v }) are lowered as 'symbolic unsupported:secondary_constructor'. Primary constructor val params now work correctly (class Node(val x: Int) generates __init__ with STORE_FIELD). Only secondary constructors remain unsupported.

title: P2: Rosetta pattern_matching uses if/else, not actual pattern matching
description: test_rosetta_pattern_matching.py: All 15 programs use if/else-if chains, not language-specific pattern matching (match/switch/when). Name implies the pattern matching feature.

title: P2: Python test_future_import_with_class has no class definition
description: test_python_frontend_execution.py::test_future_import_with_class: Name and docstring mention 'class definition' but source is just future import + arithmetic.

title: P2: test_for_loop_destructuring_execution.py is IR-only, not execution
description: File is in tests/integration/ and named 'execution' but only checks IR generation (opcodes), no VM execution via run().

title: P2: 3 Lua method tests check existence only, not correctness
description: test_lua_method_index_execution.py: test_method_call_with_args_executes, test_method_call_chained, test_method_call_executes — all only check 'r in local_vars', not that r has the expected value.

title: P2: test_type_hint_propagation.py is lowering-only, not integration
description: File is in tests/integration/ but only checks frontend lowering and type_env_builder state. No VM execution occurs.

title: Explore unifying TypedValue.type with TypeEnvironment register_types/var_types
description: TypedValue now carries runtime type alongside every value, but TypeEnvironment.register_types and var_types still store static/declared types separately. These overlap: write-time coercion (Layer 2) exists to reconcile mismatches between the two (e.g. Python float from int division when declared type is Int). If Layer 1 coercion strategies were made TypeEnvironment-aware (so result_type() returns the declared type directly), write-time coercion would become a no-op for locally-executed instructions. It would only remain necessary for LLM-produced updates. This could simplify the two-layer architecture significantly. Investigation needed: (1) how many write-time coercions actually fire in practice, (2) what it would take to thread TypeEnvironment into BinopCoercionStrategy/UnopCoercionStrategy, (3) whether the LLM path can be handled differently.

title: BinopCoercionStrategy.coerce should return tuple[TypedValue, TypedValue] not tuple[Any, Any]
description: coerce() currently strips TypedValue and returns raw values. It should preserve TypedValue wrappers so type information flows through the binop pipeline cleanly.

title: Wrap bare values in LLMPlausibleResolver._parse_llm_response with TypedValue
Refactor builtins to emit StateUpdate with heap_writes instead of direct vm.heap mutation
description: Builtins like _builtin_array_of and _builtin_object_rest currently create HeapObjects with raw field dicts directly, bypassing StateUpdate/apply_update. Refactor them to return ExecutionResult with new_objects + heap_writes, giving a uniform write path. Prerequisite: red-dragon-x2t (HeapObject.fields stores TypedValue).

title: Migrate HeapObject.fields to store TypedValue (heap Phase 2)
Replace constructor result_reg=None hack with is_ctor flag on StackFrame
description:
Current approach: _handle_call_dispatch_setup re-detects constructor calls via scope chain walk and sets result_reg=None to prevent __init__'s return value from clobbering the object address. This is fragile (had a scope chain bug) and redundant (_try_class_constructor_call already knows).

Clean approach:
1. Add is_ctor: bool = False to StackFramePush and StackFrame
2. _try_class_constructor_call sets is_ctor=True
3. _handle_return checks vm.current_frame.is_ctor — if true, produces typed(None, scalar(Void)) regardless of operands (constructors are void)
4. _handle_return_flow: skip write if return value is Void, write if None or concrete
5. Remove constructor detection from _handle_call_dispatch_setup entirely
6. Remove result_reg=None hack

Note: JS constructor explicit object return edge case tracked separately in red-dragon-4hz.

title: JS constructor explicit object return: new Foo() can return different object
description: JavaScript constructors can return a different object than 'this' if they explicitly return an object (not a primitive). This is a pathological edge case that no other supported language has. Currently not modeled — the is_ctor flag on StackFrame makes _handle_return produce Void for all constructors, swallowing any explicit return. If ever needed, the JS frontend could emit different IR for this pattern (e.g. bypass the constructor call path). Low priority — genuinely rare in real-world code.

title: Handle TypedValue in _format_val verbose logging
description: After red-dragon-132, register_writes/var_writes contain TypedValue objects. _format_val in run.py needs to handle TypedValue for verbose logging output.

title: Simplify apply_update after all handlers produce TypedValue
description: Final cleanup: once red-dragon-132 is complete and all handlers produce TypedValue directly, apply_update becomes a thin store-directly function. Remove the legacy deserialize/coerce/wrap path.

title: Migrate heap_writes values to TypedValue
description: Extend TypedValue wrapping to HeapObject.fields and heap_writes in StateUpdate. Depends on red-dragon-132 (handlers produce TypedValue) completing first. Previously tracked as red-dragon-f6i.

title: Migrate return_value in StateUpdate to TypedValue
description: After red-dragon-132 completes (handlers produce TypedValue in register_writes/var_writes), migrate return_value to TypedValue as well. Currently consumed raw by _handle_return_flow in run.py.

title: Update README for TypedValue and BinopCoercionStrategy — commit 87b1d9b
Add injectable UnopCoercionStrategy protocol, same pattern as BinopCoercionStrategy
Add BinopCoercionStrategy for C#, Kotlin, Scala, C++ string concatenation
Migrate builtins to receive TypedValue args
Migrate all executor handlers to produce TypedValue directly — remove raw-value wrapping from apply_update
Integration tests for Java BINOP string concatenation via TypedValue — commit 3d35fe0
Thread BinopCoercionStrategy through executor, use in _handle_binop — commit c34c4bf
apply_update wraps values into TypedValue, _resolve_reg auto-unwraps — commit 7d8f351
BinopCoercionStrategy protocol with Default and Java implementations — commit a6b4c5e
TypedValue dataclass with typed() and typed_from_runtime() helpers — commit c90d95a
func_signatures: convenience mutators on TypeEnvironmentBuilder
description: Add convenience methods to TypeEnvironmentBuilder for appending overloaded signatures cleanly during type inference. E.g. add_func_signature(name, sig), add_method_signature(class_type, name, sig). Replaces raw dict manipulation in _infer_const.

title: Frontend lowering-time overload resolution (optional enhancement)
description: Optional: resolve overloads at lowering time when frontend has full visibility (intra-class calls). Emit CALL_METHOD targeting a specific label. Avoids runtime resolution for the common case. Depends on execution-time resolution being complete and tested.

title: Pascal: overload resolution integration tests
description: Integration tests: different arity overloads. Pascal uses explicit overload directive. Zero LLM calls.

title: VM builtin: string/array slicing for Rust (a[1..3])
description: Rust uses range indexing (a[1..3], a[1..=3], a[..3]). Check if frontend emits slice calls and add VM support.

title: VM builtin: string/array slicing for Python (a[1:3], a[::2])
description: Python frontend emits CALL_FUNCTION('slice', start, stop, step) for subscript slices. Currently goes symbolic. Need VM builtin handling start/stop/step for strings, lists, and heap arrays.

title: VM builtin: string/list slicing for Ruby (a[1..3], a[1,2])
description: Ruby supports range-based slicing (a[1..3]) and index+length slicing (a[1,2]). Check if frontend emits slice calls and add VM support.

title: VM builtin: string/list slicing for Kotlin (substring, subList)
description: Kotlin uses method calls for slicing (substring, subList, slice). Check if these are lowered and add VM builtin support.

title: VM builtin: slice expression for Go (a[low:high])
description: Go frontend emits CALL_FUNCTION('slice', obj, low, high) for slice expressions. Currently goes symbolic. Need VM builtin for 2-arg slice (start, end) on strings, slices, and heap arrays.

title: Method chaining fully concrete across all OOP-capable languages
description:
Track remaining languages where method chaining (obj.method().method()) still produces symbolic results instead of concrete values. Currently 11/14 class-capable languages work. Remaining 3 are blocked by independent issues:
- C++: stack construction doesn't trigger constructor (red-dragon-ng8)
- Lua: table-based OOP not tracked by VM (red-dragon-0vp)  
- Scala: implicit return from expression blocks (red-dragon-2j7)

This issue is resolved when all three blockers are fixed and the _CHAINING_SYMBOLIC_LANGUAGES skip list in test_rosetta_method_chaining.py is empty.

title: Lua table-based OOP method chaining produces symbolic results
description: Lua has no native class system. The Rosetta method chaining test uses a table-based OOP pattern (Counter.new(), Counter.increment(self)) with explicit self-passing. This pattern produces symbolic results because the VM doesn't recognise the table-as-object convention. The Lua frontend would need to detect self-returning table methods and track the table identity through the call chain. This may be a lower priority since Lua's OOP is purely conventional, not language-level.

title: C++ stack-constructed objects don't trigger implicit constructor call
description: When C++ declares an object on the stack (e.g. `Counter counter;`), no constructor is invoked. Unlike `new Counter()` which emits NEW_OBJECT + CALL_METHOD __init__, stack construction has no equivalent lowering. This means field initializers are never applied and method chaining on stack-constructed objects produces symbolic results. The frontend needs to detect variable declarations whose type is a known class and emit an implicit constructor call.
close_reason: Closed

title: Scala array indexing emits CALL_FUNCTION instead of LOAD_INDEX
description: Scala array access (e.g. arr(0)) is lowered as CALL_FUNCTION instead of LOAD_INDEX, because Scala's tree-sitter grammar represents array indexing as a function call. This causes the Rosetta array_accumulate test to skip for Scala. The frontend should detect the array-access pattern and emit LOAD_INDEX instead.

title: PHP lower_php_enum_case uses bare constants.PARAM_SELF in STORE_FIELD
description: In interpreter/frontends/php/declarations.py, lower_php_enum_case emits STORE_FIELD with constants.PARAM_SELF ('self') as the object operand instead of using a register from LOAD_VAR. This is the same bare-string pattern that was fixed for property declarations in the B2 field initializer work. The enum case should either LOAD_VAR the enum object or use the class-level register.

title: Lua method chaining: dynamic table initialization not tracked through VM
description: Lua models objects as tables created in factory functions (local self = {count = 0}), lowered as NEW_OBJECT + STORE_INDEX. Method calls are positional (Counter.increment(counter)) not method syntax. The VM can't track heap objects through dynamic table initialization and function-based dispatch. This is an architectural limitation — no class/constructor mechanism for Lua to hook into. Unlike the C++/PHP bugs, this requires deeper VM support for table-based OOP patterns. See _CHAINING_SYMBOLIC_LANGUAGES in test_rosetta_method_chaining.py.

title: Wrap remaining raw class_name strings in ScalarType() at seed call sites
description: ~25 call sites across 10 OOP frontends still pass raw class_name strings (from ctx.node_text()) to seed_register_type, seed_param_type, seed_var_type. These should be wrapped in ScalarType(class_name) to complete the string→TypeExpr migration. Covers this/self seeding and cast expression handlers in JS, Java, C#, C++, Kotlin, Scala, PHP, and expression files.

title: Migrate existing seed call sites from string to TypeExpr
description: ~100 existing call sites across 15 frontends pass raw strings to seed_register_type, seed_var_type, and seed_param_type. Migrate them to pass TypeExpr objects directly (ScalarType, metatype, etc.) instead of going through parse_type() roundtrips. The seed methods already accept str | TypeExpr, so this is incremental.

title: Method overload resolution via subtype matching
Assignment compatibility for reference types
description:
TypeResolver.resolve_assignment() currently only handles primitive coercions (Int→Float, Bool→Int). For reference types, Animal a = new Dog() should be a no-op (no coercion needed) when Dog <: Animal via is_subtype_expr().

Currently this case is handled implicitly (VM doesn't coerce objects), but explicit subtype checking would enable:
- Compile-time warnings for incompatible assignments
- Covariant container checks (List<Dog> assignable to List<Animal> with proper variance)

Lower priority than type narrowing — the VM works without this today.

title: Method chaining: return-this produces symbolic for 7 languages
description: Method chaining (return this/self) produces symbolic results for C++, C#, Java, Kotlin, Lua, PHP, Scala. The VM doesn't resolve 'return this' back to the object reference correctly in these frontends. Discovered during method chaining Rosetta test.

title: Rosetta: string length test
description: Dropped: VM can't resolve len()/strlen()/.length/.len()/#s/Length() to concrete values for most languages (only Python, Go, Lua work). Needs built-in function resolution in VM executor.

title: Rosetta: default parameters test
description: Skipped by user. Default parameter binding produces symbolic results for most languages during VM execution. Needs VM-level support for default param resolution.

title: Rosetta: multiple return values test
description: Deferred by user. Needs separate work to handle: C/C++ global array from void fn, Go tuple return, Lua multi-return unpacking, Pascal var params, Scala arr(i)->CALL_FUNCTION. Only 9/15 languages produce correct execution results.

title: Code review: remaining for-loop mutations (~15 in vm.py/executor.py/cfg.py)
description: From Programming Patterns Audit #1 (2026-03-07). ~15 for-loop mutations remain in vm.py, executor.py, cfg.py, type_graph.py — left as-is because they are inherently imperative shell. Also ~120 defensive None checks (systemic/low) and magic strings (incremental). See memory/code_review_audit1.md.

title: COBOL ref_mod: UNSTRING INTO targets — WS-FIELD(start:length) as receiving field in UNSTRING
description: Reference modification on UNSTRING INTO receiving fields is not implemented. COBOL allows UNSTRING WS-SRC DELIMITED SPACE INTO WS-A(2:3) WS-B, writing each split part into a substring of the target field (write-back via __string_splice). The Java bridge serializeUnstring() serializes INTO targets as plain strings. The Python UnstringStatement.into is a list[str]. The lowering writes the full field unconditionally.
acceptance_criteria: Java bridge serializes each INTO target as a ref-mod-aware operand object. UnstringStatement.into becomes list[RefModOperand]. Lowering applies __string_splice when ref_mod is present on an INTO target. Integration test: UNSTRING WS-SRC DELIMITED SPACE INTO WS-A(2:3), assert correct bytes written into substring range of WS-A.

title: SUBTRACT CORRESPONDING ON SIZE ERROR: overflow detection not implemented
description: SUBTRACT CORRESPONDING (COBOL SUBTRACT ... CORRESPONDING ... FROM ...) has no ON SIZE ERROR / NOT ON SIZE ERROR support. SUBTRACT CORRESPONDING is not yet implemented in the lowering layer; when it is, overflow detection must be included. Each corresponding field pair must be checked against its target type descriptor (signed fields check both bounds; unsigned only upper bound). Overflow OR'd across all pairs before a single BranchIf. StatementSerializer.java serializeSubtract() needs onSizeErrorPhrase/notOnSizeErrorPhrase for the CORRESPONDING variant. Files: interpreter/cobol/lower_arithmetic.py (lower_subtract_corresponding, to be created), proleap-bridge/src/main/java/org/reddragon/bridge/StatementSerializer.java. Related: red-dragon-4q25.5 (ArithmeticStatement ON SIZE ERROR pattern), red-dragon-d1w8 (ADD CORRESPONDING ON SIZE ERROR).

title: ADD CORRESPONDING ON SIZE ERROR: overflow detection not implemented
description: ADD CORRESPONDING (COBOL ADD ... CORRESPONDING ... TO ...) has no ON SIZE ERROR / NOT ON SIZE ERROR support. ADD CORRESPONDING is not yet implemented in the lowering layer; when it is, overflow detection must be included. Each corresponding field pair must be checked independently against its target type descriptor, with overflow OR'd across all pairs before branching. StatementSerializer.java serializeAdd() also needs onSizeErrorPhrase/notOnSizeErrorPhrase wired for the CORRESPONDING variant. Files: interpreter/cobol/lower_arithmetic.py (lower_arithmetic_corresponding, to be created), proleap-bridge/src/main/java/org/reddragon/bridge/StatementSerializer.java. Related: red-dragon-4q25.5 (ArithmeticStatement ON SIZE ERROR pattern).

title: SUBTRACT CORRESPONDING ON SIZE ERROR not implemented
description: SUBTRACT CORRESPONDING is not yet implemented in RedDragon. When it is implemented, it must support ON SIZE ERROR / NOT ON SIZE ERROR clauses per COBOL standard. Filed as a tracking issue so the overflow clause is not forgotten when SUBTRACT CORRESPONDING is eventually implemented. Related: red-dragon-4q25.5 (ADD/SUBTRACT/MULTIPLY/DIVIDE ON SIZE ERROR).

title: ADD CORRESPONDING ON SIZE ERROR not implemented
description: ADD CORRESPONDING is not yet implemented in RedDragon. When it is implemented, it must support ON SIZE ERROR / NOT ON SIZE ERROR clauses per COBOL standard. Filed as a tracking issue so the overflow clause is not forgotten when ADD CORRESPONDING is eventually implemented. Related: red-dragon-4q25.5 (ADD/SUBTRACT/MULTIPLY/DIVIDE ON SIZE ERROR).

title: instructions.py: remove redundant result_reg redeclarations in subclasses
description:
File: interpreter/instructions.py
Lines: ~203, 219, 282, 301, 330, 353, 376, 402, 426, 453, 496, 519, 575, 616, 638, 654, 824, 843

After red-dragon-4ei7 (result_reg promoted to InstructionBase), 18 subclasses still explicitly redeclare result_reg: Register = NO_REGISTER. These are now redundant — Python dataclass inheritance deduplicates them. They compile correctly but mislead readers into thinking result_reg is subclass-specific for some classes and not others.

Fix: remove the standalone result_reg: Register = NO_REGISTER line from the ~18 subclasses that have it (Const, LoadVar, Binop, Unop, CallFunction, CallMethod, etc.). Keep result_reg fields that are semantically different (different default or different type).

Note: do NOT remove result_reg from subclasses where it appears inside the compat block — those were already removed in red-dragon-4ei7.

Epic: red-dragon-r32l

title: Phase 3 gate upgrade: typeCheckingMode standard + strip per-file pragmas
description:
Once all 89 demoted files are promoted to # pyright: standard, execute Task 18 from the type-hints plan (docs/superpowers/plans/2026-03-31-type-hints.md):
1. Upgrade pyrightconfig.json to typeCheckingMode: standard
2. Strip all per-file # pyright: standard comments (now redundant)
3. Fix any newly surfaced errors
4. Update .claude/core/workflow.md gate comment

Blocked by: fix red-dragon-hzmm (str→Register in instruction constructors, ~50 frontend files), red-dragon-1vgf (emit_func_ref str vs Register), red-dragon-y5bm (push_loop str vs CodeLabel), red-dragon-4ei7 (InstructionBase missing attrs).

Current state: 156 files promoted, 89 demoted. Run 'grep -rL # pyright: standard interpreter/ mcp_server/ --include=*.py | grep -v __pycache__ | grep -v __init__' to see which files remain.

title: pre_triage: Any in TypedValue.value and typed_value.py helpers — raw VM runtime values
description:
File: interpreter/types/typed_value.py
Symbols:
  - TypedValue.value: Any (line 34)
  - runtime_type_name(val: Any) (line 20)
  - typed(value: Any, ...) (line 38)
  - unwrap(val: Any) -> Any (line 43)
  - unwrap_locals(local_vars: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any] (line 50)
  - typed_from_runtime(value: Any) (line 55)
Context: All Any uses here represent raw Python objects flowing through the VM at runtime: int, str, float, bool, list, dict, custom VM objects (Pointer, SymbolicValue, etc.). The union of all possible VM values is very wide. A domain type like VmValue = Union[int, str, float, bool, list, dict, object] could be explored, but the heterogeneous nature makes it a significant effort.
Epic: red-dragon-r32l

title: pre_triage: Callable[[Any], Any] in NullTypeResolver.resolve_assignment — coercion function over raw VM values
description:
File: interpreter/types/null_type_resolver.py
Symbol: NullTypeResolver.resolve_assignment() -> Callable[[Any], Any]
Context: Inherits from TypeResolver. Returns _identity (the identity function), which applies no coercion. Both input and output are raw VM runtime values (int, str, list, etc.). Parallel to red-dragon-isoc in TypeResolver.
Epic: red-dragon-r32l

title: pre_triage: Callable[[Any], Any] in TypeResolver.resolve_assignment — coercion function over raw VM values
description:
File: interpreter/types/type_resolver.py
Symbol: TypeResolver.resolve_assignment() -> Callable[[Any], Any]
Context: The return type is a coercion function applied to raw VM runtime values. Both the input and output are untyped Python objects (int, str, list, custom VM objects). A domain type like CoercionFn = Callable[[object], object] or a Protocol could replace Any here.
Epic: red-dragon-r32l

title: What is the type of a coercer argument? Define RuntimeValue TypeAlias?
description:
File: interpreter/types/coercion/conversion_result.py, interpreter/types/coercion/conversion_rules.py, interpreter/types/coercion/default_conversion_rules.py, interpreter/types/coercion/identity_conversion_rules.py
Symbol: Callable[[Any], Any] — _identity, _to_float, _to_int, _truncate_to_int, left_coercer, right_coercer, coerce_assignment return type
Context: All coercer callables accept and return heterogeneous runtime values (int, float, bool, str, etc.) dispatched at runtime based on type ontology. Currently typed as Callable[[Any], Any]. A RuntimeValue TypeAlias (Union of all concrete runtime primitives, or a Protocol) could replace the Any parameters, making the boundary explicit and enabling stricter checking at call sites.
Epic: red-dragon-r32l

title: Migrate Definition.variable / Use.variable to StorageIdentifier
description: Change Definition.variable and Use.variable from str to StorageIdentifier. Clean up all downstream dicts (produced_from, defs_by_var) to use StorageIdentifier keys. Simplify _trace_to_named_vars: isinstance(reg, VarName) for leaves, isinstance(reg, Register) for intermediates — eliminates _is_temporary_register string heuristic. Fix interprocedural/summaries.py comparison sites. Update tests in test_dataflow.py and test_interprocedural_types.py. Depends on the StorageIdentifier + used_storage/defined_storage issue.
close_reason: Definition.variable and Use.variable changed from str to StorageIdentifier. _defs_of and _uses_of now delegate to instruction.writes()/reads(). Removed _VALUE_PRODUCERS, _VAR_DEFINERS constants (dead code). Removed _is_temporary_register string heuristic — isinstance(id, VarName) for leaves, isinstance(id, Register) for intermediates. All internal dicts (produced_from, defs_by_var, gen_map, local_defs) use StorageIdentifier keys. Added LoadVar.reads() override. 2 test files updated. 13,108 tests passing.

title: StorageIdentifier protocol + used_storage/defined_storage on instruction classes
description:
1. Create interpreter/storage_identifier.py with @runtime_checkable StorageIdentifier protocol (value: str, is_present() -> bool, __str__, __hash__). Both VarName and Register already satisfy it structurally.
2. Add used_storage() -> list[StorageIdentifier] method to InstructionBase (default empty) and override on every typed instruction class to return the typed Register/VarName fields that represent storage reads.
3. Add defined_storage() -> StorageIdentifier | None method — returns result_reg (Register) for value producers, name (VarName) for STORE_VAR/DECL_VAR, None for instructions that don't define storage.
4. Rewrite _defs_of and _uses_of in dataflow.py to call these methods instead of parsing operands.
This decouples dataflow analysis from the stringified operands property.
close_reason: Created StorageIdentifier protocol (interpreter/storage_identifier.py). Added reads() and writes() methods to all 30 instruction classes in instructions.py. Fixed 33 bare-string value_reg sites across 18 frontend files. Reverted _defs_of/_uses_of rewrite — blocked by v11s (remaining bare-string Register fields on obj_reg, left, right, etc.). 13,108 tests passing.

title: Introduce ModulePath domain type for ImportRef.module_path
description: ImportRef.module_path (interpreter/project/types.py) is raw str representing module import paths like 'os.path', './utils', 'com.example.Utils'. Flows through 57 sites in resolver.py and imports.py. Language-agnostic opaque wrapper — no parsing, just identity and display. Prevents accidental comparison with file paths.

title: Push VarName into FunctionEntry.params and FunctionSignature.params name component
description: DEFERRED: VarName.__eq__ rejects str, so migrating func_params/FunctionEntry.params/FunctionSignature.params breaks every 'param in SELF_PARAM_NAMES' check and ~10 test assertions silently. The current VarName(params[i]) wrapping at call sites in calls.py works correctly. Blast radius is disproportionate to value. title: Design null-object pattern for failed function lookups across all dispatch strategies
description:
Currently, function lookup failures are handled inconsistently across dispatch strategies:

1. Builtins: _try_builtin_call returns ExecutionResult.not_handled() when func_name not in TABLE (calls.py:76-77)
2. Scope chain: returns empty string func_val when name not in any frame's local_vars (calls.py:309-314)
3. Class methods: returns empty list from registry.class_methods.get(class_name, {}).get(method_name, []) (calls.py:464-465)
4. Method builtins: returns None from METHOD_TABLE.get(method_name) (calls.py:477)
5. Parent chain: falls through with empty func_label (calls.py:534-554)
6. Final fallback: call_resolver.resolve_call / resolve_method — LLM/symbolic (calls.py:317, 517)

Each failure is checked differently (not in dict, empty string, empty list, None, not_handled). A unified null-object pattern would:
- Define a NullCallable / NOT_A_BUILTIN sentinel distinct from UNCOMPUTABLE
- Make every lookup return a callable (null or real) instead of None/empty
- Unify the three-way dispatch: not-found → try-next-strategy, found-but-uncomputable → symbolic, found-and-computed → concrete

Files: interpreter/handlers/calls.py (primary), interpreter/vm/builtins.py (TABLE/METHOD_TABLE), interpreter/registry.py (class_methods, func_refs).

This is a dispatch architecture improvement, not blocking FuncName migration. FuncName accessors use Optional for now.

title: Rust frontend: migrate Box/Option from CALL_FUNCTION with embedded type params to CALL_CTOR
description:
Rust frontend emits CALL_FUNCTION 'Box[Node]' with generic type params embedded in func_name string. The handler in calls.py:272 has to strip them with split('[')[0]. This should use CALL_CTOR (CallCtorFunction) instead, which has a dedicated type_hint field for the parameterized type.

Current: CallFunction(func_name='Box[Node]', ...) → handler does func_name.split('[')[0] to get 'Box'
Target: CallCtorFunction(func_name='Box', type_hint=ParameterizedType('Box', [scalar('Node')]), ...)

Files: interpreter/frontends/rust/declarations.py (Box::new and Some emission), interpreter/handlers/calls.py:272 (remove split hack).

Blocking FuncName clean-name invariant — FuncName must store clean names only, no embedded type parameters.
close_reason: Dead split hack removed. Frontend already emits clean FuncName. Filed 0tsw for CallCtorFunction follow-up.

- register_source_var: dict[str, str] (line 198) — maps register name to source variable name. str(name) at line 581.
- var_array_element_types: dict[str, TypeExpr] (line 191) — maps variable name to array element type. str(name) at lines 586-588, 614.
- var_tuple_element_types: dict[str, dict[int, TypeExpr]] (line 195) — maps variable name to tuple element types. str(name) at lines 591-593, 617.
- store_var_type / lookup_var_type: methods on _InferenceContext (lines 207, 224) that take str name param. str(name) at lines 582, 610.

All 4 dicts should change key type from str to VarName. The store_var_type/lookup_var_type method signatures should accept VarName. The scoped_var_types (line 188) which underlies lookup_var_type is dict[str, dict[str, TypeExpr]] — outer key is scope, inner key is var name — inner key should become VarName.

No external consumers of these dicts — they are internal to _InferenceContext. Low risk, self-contained change. No Pydantic models involved (pure dataclass).

Constraint: the TypeEnvironment.scoped_var_types in type_environment.py also uses str var name keys and feeds into lookup_var_type. That may need updating too — check during implementation.

Related: red-dragon-w667 (VarName epic), completed issues b9cd/jdx0/90z9.

title: Investigate LLMPlausibleResolver gaps: orphan HeapWrites, missing NewObject, type_hint inference
description:
LLMPlausibleResolver (interpreter/vm/unresolved_call.py:185-204) emits HeapWrite without companion NewObject entries. This triggers the defensive heap_ensure fallback in apply_update (vm.py:251-252) which creates untyped HeapObject(type_hint=UNKNOWN).

Known gaps:
1. HeapWrite without NewObject — LLM JSON response doesn't include object creation, only field writes. The address may not exist in the heap.
2. No type_hint inference — LLM doesn't provide type info for heap objects it references.
3. obj_addr values from LLM are arbitrary strings — no validation against existing heap state.
4. The LLM prompt template (lines 145-170) doesn't instruct the LLM to distinguish new objects from field updates on existing objects.

Potential fixes:
- Add NewObject entries to the LLM response schema
- Infer type_hint from context (e.g., the function return type or argument types)
- Validate obj_addr against existing heap before creating orphan objects

Files: interpreter/vm/unresolved_call.py (LLMPlausibleResolver._parse_llm_response, _build_prompt), interpreter/vm/vm.py (apply_update heap_ensure fallback).

Low urgency — the defensive fallback works correctly, just creates untyped objects.

title: Rust frontend: migrate Box/Option from CallFunction to CallCtorFunction for typed constructor dispatch
description:
Box::new and Some are lowered as CallFunction in interpreter/frontends/rust/expressions.py (lines 85, 107). They should use CallCtorFunction with a type_hint field for proper parameterized type propagation.

Current: CallFunction(func_name=FuncName('Box'), args=...) — handler infers type via parse_type(str(base_name)) which returns scalar('Box')
Target: CallCtorFunction(func_name=FuncName('Box'), type_hint=ParameterizedType('Box', [inferred_element_type]), args=...)

The split('[') hack was already removed (n9tr). This is about using the right opcode for constructor dispatch, enabling typed Box[T] and Option[T] propagation through the type system.

Files: interpreter/frontends/rust/expressions.py (_lower_box_new, _lower_some). Low urgency — current approach works, just uses the wrong opcode.

title: Investigate: runtime string → FieldName impedance mismatch at indirect handlers
description:
LoadFieldIndirect and StoreIndirect handlers convert runtime string values (from registers) to FieldName for heap lookups. This is a boundary crossing between the runtime value domain (str in registers) and the heap key domain (FieldName).

Current flow (post-FieldName migration):
1. StoreField: frontend produces FieldName → handler writes to heap with FieldName key
2. LoadFieldIndirect: register holds runtime str → handler wraps FieldName(str) → looks up heap
3. StoreIndirect: Pointer.offset (int) → str(offset) → FieldName(str, INDEX) → HeapWrite
4. _common.py spread: range(len(fields)) → str(i) → FieldName(str(i), INDEX) → fields lookup

The wrapping at step 2-4 is a necessary impedance mismatch — runtime values are strings, heap keys are FieldName. Not a bug, but worth investigating whether:
- CONST instructions could produce FieldName values directly (eliminates wrap at LoadFieldIndirect)
- Pointer.offset could store FieldName instead of int (eliminates double str() conversion)
- Both would mean FieldName becomes a runtime value type, which has architectural implications

Files: interpreter/handlers/memory.py (LoadFieldIndirect ~line 237, StoreIndirect ~line 289), interpreter/handlers/_common.py (spread args ~line 27), interpreter/vm/vm.py (alias write ~line 283).

This may not need fixing — the current wrap-at-boundary pattern is correct and explicit. Filed for future analysis only.

title: Introduce Address domain type for HeapWrite.obj_addr and heap address fields
description:
HeapWrite.obj_addr is str representing a heap object address (e.g. 'obj_0', 'arr_3', 'mem_0'). Also used as keys in vm.heap: dict[str, HeapObject], Pointer.base: str, and NewObject.addr: str. Should be a domain type Address(value: str) to prevent interchange with other strings.

Files: interpreter/vm/vm_types.py (HeapWrite.obj_addr, NewObject.addr, Pointer.base), interpreter/vm/vm.py (vm.heap keys). ~50 construction sites in handlers/builtins + tests.

Low urgency — addresses are already structurally distinct (prefixed with obj_/arr_/mem_/sym_) and not easily confused with other string types.
close_reason: Address domain type complete: VMState accessors (heap_get/set/contains/ensure/items/keys, region_get/set) as permanent API. vm._heap: dict[Address, HeapObject], vm._regions: dict[Address, bytearray] — both private. Pointer.base, HeapWrite.obj_addr, NewObject.addr, RegionWrite.region_addr all Address. NullHeapObject for miss returns. No str bridge. 13,106 tests passing.

title: Migrate SymbolTable and FunctionRegistry to domain type keys
description:
SymbolTable and FunctionRegistry use raw str keys for all name types:

- ClassInfo.fields: dict[str, FieldInfo] — should be dict[FieldName, FieldInfo]
- ClassInfo.methods: dict[str, FunctionInfo] — should be dict[FuncName, FunctionInfo] (depends on cnz9)
- ClassInfo.constants: dict[str, str] — constant names, may stay str
- ClassInfo.name: str — should be class name domain type
- ClassInfo.parents: tuple[str, ...] — class names
- SymbolTable.classes: dict[str, ClassInfo] — class names
- SymbolTable.functions: dict[str, FunctionInfo] — function names
- SymbolTable.resolve_field(class_name: str, field_name: str) — both params need domain types
- FunctionRegistry.func_params: dict[CodeLabel, list[str]] — param names should be list[VarName]
- FunctionRegistry.class_methods: dict[str, dict[str, list[CodeLabel]]] — class name → method name
- FunctionRegistry.classes: dict[str, CodeLabel] — class names
- FunctionRegistry.class_parents: dict[str, list[str]] — class names
- FunctionRegistry.func_refs: dict[str, FuncRef] — function names

Files: interpreter/frontends/symbol_table.py, interpreter/registry.py.

Currently the boundary between domain-typed instructions and str-keyed symbol tables requires str() unwrapping. This is the general pattern — not specific to any one domain type. Depends on j0h1 (FieldName), cnz9 (FuncName), w667 (VarName) being complete.

Low urgency — current str() unwrapping at the boundary is correct and explicit.

title: Review Symbolic.hint representation
description: Symbolic.hint is currently str, holding freeform descriptive text ('__param__x', 'unknown_import'). May benefit from a structured hint type if patterns emerge, or may be fine as freeform str. Placeholder for future review.

title: Review LoadRegion/WriteRegion.length representation
description: LoadRegion.length and WriteRegion.length are currently int, holding byte counts for COBOL byte-addressed memory. May benefit from a ByteLength type for clarity, or may be fine as plain int given COBOL-only usage. Placeholder for future review.

title: Review Const.value representation
description: Const.value is currently str, holding polymorphic literal values ('42', 'True', '"hello"', '<function:fib@func_fib_0>'). May benefit from a typed ConstValue ADT, or may be fine as str since parsing happens at VM runtime. Placeholder for future review.
